---
title: "Adult Pilot Study - MIA"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true            # Enable Table of Contents
    toc_float: true      # Makes the Table of Contents float (optional)
date: "06-03-2025"
---




# Function to install/load packages:
```{r load_packages function, include=FALSE}
load_packages <- function(packages) {
  sapply(packages, function(pkg) {
    if (!require(pkg, character.only = TRUE)) {
      install.packages(pkg, dependencies = TRUE)
      library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
    }
  })
}

packages <- c("purrr", "dplyr", "tidyr", "knitr", "magrittr", "reshape2", 
              "tidyverse", "ggplot2", "RColorBrewer", "stringr",
              "rstatix", "ggpubr", "ggsignif", "FSA",  
              "readxl", "viridis", "patchwork",  "readr",
              "ggfortify", "factoextra", "pheatmap",
              "cowplot","pheatmap","psych","cowplot")

load_packages(packages)
library(gt)
getwd()

```


# Import data
```{r, include=FALSE}

cl_df_long <- read_excel("04-antigen_specific_MIA_data.xlsx")


```

# How many samples were out-of-range (OOR)?
```{r, echo=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(gt)

# Summarize OOR values by target and Sampling_Method using the long-format df
target_summary <- cl_df_long %>%
  group_by(target, Sampling_Method) %>%
  summarise(
    OOR_High = sum(limits == "OOR >", na.rm = TRUE),
    OOR_Low  = sum(limits == "OOR <", na.rm = TRUE),
    total_samples = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    OOR_High_Percent = (OOR_High / total_samples) * 100,
    OOR_Low_Percent  = (OOR_Low / total_samples) * 100,
    High_Format = paste0(OOR_High, " (", round(OOR_High_Percent, 1), "%)"),
    Low_Format  = paste0(OOR_Low, " (", round(OOR_Low_Percent, 1), "%)")
  )

# Reshape to wider format with Sampling_Method as column groups
target_table <- target_summary %>%
  select(target, Sampling_Method, High_Format, Low_Format) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Sampling_Method, values_from = c(High_Format, Low_Format)) %>%
  rename_with(~ gsub("High_Format_", "High_", .x), starts_with("High_Format")) %>%
  rename_with(~ gsub("Low_Format_", "Low_", .x), starts_with("Low_Format"))

# Create the gt table. Adjust the spanner labels as needed.
gt_table1 <- target_table %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "target") %>%
  tab_header(
    title = md("Out of Range (OOR) Values (Count and Percentage)")
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = md("**MTS**"),  
    columns = c(High_MTS_Professional, Low_MTS_Professional, High_MTS_Self, Low_MTS_Self)
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = md("**NPS**"),  
    columns = c(High_NPS_Professional, Low_NPS_Professional)
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = md("**MLF**"),  
    columns = c(High_MLF_Professional, Low_MLF_Professional, High_MLF_Self, Low_MLF_Self)
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = md("**OPS**"),  
    columns = c(High_OPS_Professional, Low_OPS_Professional)
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(
    label = md("**Saliva**"),  
    columns = c(High_Saliva_Professional, Low_Saliva_Professional, High_Saliva_Self, Low_Saliva_Self)
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    High_MTS_Self = "Self (OOR >)",     Low_MTS_Self = "Self (OOR <)",
    High_MTS_Professional = "Professional (OOR >)", Low_MTS_Professional = "Professional (OOR <)",
    High_NPS_Professional = "Professional (OOR >)",   Low_NPS_Professional = "Professional (OOR <)",
    High_MLF_Self = "Self (OOR >)",      Low_MLF_Self = "Self (OOR <)",
    High_MLF_Professional = "Professional (OOR >)",    Low_MLF_Professional = "Professional (OOR <)",
    High_OPS_Professional = "Professional (OOR >)",    Low_OPS_Professional = "Professional (OOR <)",
    High_Saliva_Professional = "Professional (OOR >)", Low_Saliva_Professional = "Professional (OOR <)",
    High_Saliva_Self = "Self (OOR >)",   Low_Saliva_Self = "Self (OOR <)"
  ) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = 12,
    heading.title.font.size = 14
  )

# Print the table
gt_table1
# save the table
gtsave(gt_table1, "MIA_censoring_table.html")
```

# Data processing (remove samples that fell OOR)
```{r}
cl_df_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(concentration)) %>%        # keep only samples with data
  group_by(Sampling_Method, target) %>%        # group by site and analyte
  summarise(n_samples = n(), .groups = "drop")

```



# Statistics (Prof)
```{r}
library(rstatix)
pro_df <- cl_df_long %>%
  filter(Sampling_Strategy == "Professional", !is.na(concentration))

pro_df <- pro_df %>%
  mutate(log_conc = log10(concentration))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility

# Step 1: We do this just for MTS and MLF since they have two samples taken (i.e. from the left and right nostrils), Because these were not statistically different, we take one sample randomly to ensure complete cases
mts_mlf_collapsed <- pro_df %>%
  filter(Sample_Type %in% c("MTS", "MLF")) %>%
  group_by(ID, target, Sample_Type) %>%
  slice_sample(n = 1) %>%
  ungroup()


other_samples <- pro_df %>%
  filter(!Sample_Type %in% c("MTS", "MLF"))

collapsed_df <- bind_rows(mts_mlf_collapsed, other_samples)
collapsed_df <- collapsed_df %>%
  mutate(log_conc = log10(concentration)) # log10 transformation
# transform data to wide format
wide_df <- collapsed_df  %>%
  select(ID, Sample_Type, target, log_conc) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Sample_Type, values_from = log_conc)


collapsed_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(concentration)) %>%
  count(target, Sample_Type, name = "n_samples")
library(purrr)
# the pairs that we will apply the test
sample_pairs <- list(
  c("NPS", "MLF"),
  c("NPS", "MTS"),
  c("NPS", "OPS"),
  c("NPS", "Saliva")
)

stat_results <- map_dfr(sample_pairs, function(pair) {
  wide_df %>%
    group_by(target) %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[pair[1]]]) & !is.na(.data[[pair[2]]])) %>%
    summarise(
      p = wilcox.test(.data[[pair[1]]], .data[[pair[2]]], paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)$p.value,
      group1 = pair[1],
      group2 = pair[2],
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}) %>%
  mutate(p.adj = p.adjust(p, method = "bonferroni"),
         p.adj.signif = case_when(
           p.adj < 0.001 ~ "***",
           p.adj < 0.01 ~ "**",
           p.adj < 0.05 ~ "*",
           TRUE ~ "ns"
         ),
         y.position = 5.5  # adjust as needed
  )

# save results
#writexl::write_xlsx(stat_results, "Specific-Ab-Wilcox-results.xlsx")
```



# Preparations for visualizing the results
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6, echo=FALSE}
# Define custom colors for our Sample Types
custom_colors <- c("NPS" = "#A2D2DF", 
                   "MTS" = "#F6EFBD",       
                   "MLF" = "#FFBD73",
                   "OPS" = "#BC7C7C",
                   "Saliva" = "#8ABFA3")

collapsed_df.nona <- collapsed_df %>% 
  filter(!is.na(concentration)) # removed missing values

```
# Visualize the Wilcoxon Paired test results & concentrations
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}


# Define the function that processes one target:
plot_target <- function(target_name, data, sample_types = c("NPS", "MTS", "MLF", "OPS", "Saliva")) {
  
  # 1. Filter the data for the given target
  df_target <- data %>% filter(target == target_name)
  
  # For non-"PT" targets, find common IDs and filter to balanced data.
  # For "PT", keep all samples.
  if(target_name != "PT") {
    common_ids <- df_target %>% 
      filter(Sample_Type %in% sample_types) %>% 
      group_by(ID) %>% 
      summarise(n = n_distinct(Sample_Type)) %>% 
      filter(n == length(sample_types)) %>% 
      pull(ID)
    
    df_balanced <- df_target %>%
      filter(ID %in% common_ids, Sample_Type %in% sample_types) %>%
      mutate(log_conc = log10(concentration))
  } else {
    # For target "PT", use all available data and create log10 values.
    df_balanced <- df_target %>% 
      mutate(log_conc = log10(concentration))
  }
  
  # Base plot: boxplot with jittered points
  p <- ggplot(df_balanced, aes(x = Sample_Type, y = log_conc, fill = Sample_Type)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.5, outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.4, position = position_jitter(width = 0.15)) +
    labs(
  title = paste("Target:", target_name),
  y = ifelse(target_name == "PT", 
             "Log10 Concentration (IU/mL)", 
             "Log10 Concentration (AU/mL)"), x=NULL
) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("NPS" = "#A2D2DF", 
                                 "MTS" = "#F6EFBD",       
                                 "MLF" = "#FFBD73",
                                 "OPS" = "#BC7C7C",
                                 "Saliva" = "#8ABFA3")) +
    theme_bw(base_size=25)+theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 25),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 22),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 22),
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 25),  # facet labels
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 25),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 24),
  )
   
  
  # If the target is not "PT", add paired Wilcoxon test comparisons.
  if(target_name != "PT"){
    my_comparisons <- list(c("NPS", "MTS"), 
                           c("NPS", "MLF"),
                           c("NPS", "OPS"),
                           c("NPS", "Saliva"))
    
    p <- p + stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons,
                                method = "wilcox.test",
                                paired = TRUE,
                                label = "p.signif",
                                tip.length = 0.01)
  }
  
  return(p)
}


# Get all distinct targets:
targets <- unique(collapsed_df.nona$target)

# Generate a list of plots, one per target
plot_list <- lapply(targets, function(t) plot_target(t, collapsed_df.nona))

# Combine all plots into one figure with one common legend at the bottom
combined_plot <- ggarrange(plotlist = plot_list,
                           ncol = length(plot_list),
                           common.legend = TRUE,
                           legend = "bottom")
# Display the combined plot
print(combined_plot)

# Save the plot
ggsave(
  filename = "Target-specific-Ab-plot-prof.tiff",
  plot = combined_plot,
  device = "tiff",
  width = 12,      # width in inches
  height = 9,      # height in inches
  dpi = 300        # dots per inch for good resolution
)


```
# Concentration ranges (per sample type and target)
```{r, echo=FALSE}
library(stringr)
library(gt)

# Summarize the data
summary_table <- cl_df_long %>%
  group_by(target, Sampling_Method) %>%  # Group by Target and Result (SARS, Pertussis, etc.)
  summarise(
    Min_Concentration = min(concentration, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_Concentration = max(concentration, na.rm = TRUE),
    Unit = first(unit)  # Assuming Unit is the same within groups
  ) %>%
  ungroup()



library(dplyr)
library(gt)
library(stringr)

# Extract Sampling Method from Sampling_Method
summary_table <- summary_table %>%
  mutate(
    `Sample Type` = str_extract(Sampling_Method, "^[^_]+"),
    `Sampling Strategy` = str_extract(Sampling_Method, "(?<=_)[^_]+$")  # Extracts second part after "_"# Extracts MLF, MTS, etc.
  )

# Create GT table with dynamic row grouping
gt_table <- summary_table %>%
  select(`Sample Type`,`Sampling Strategy`, target, Min_Concentration, Max_Concentration, Unit) %>%
  arrange(`Sampling Strategy`, target) %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "Target") %>%
  tab_header(title = "Concentration Ranges per Sampling Method") %>%
  # Dynamically create row groups for each sampling method
  tab_row_group(label = md("**MLF**"), rows = `Sample Type` == "MLF") %>%
  tab_row_group(label = md("**MTS**"), rows = `Sample Type` == "MTS") %>%
  tab_row_group(label = md("**NPS**"), rows = `Sample Type` == "NPS") %>%
  tab_row_group(label = md("**OPS**"), rows = `Sample Type` == "OPS") %>%
  tab_row_group(label = md("**Saliva**"), rows = `Sample Type` == "Saliva") %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(Min_Concentration, Max_Concentration),
    decimals = 2
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    target = "Antigen",
    Min_Concentration = "Min Conc.",
    Max_Concentration = "Max Conc.",
    Unit = "Unit"
  )

# Display GT table
gt_table

# save table
# gtsave(gt_table, "MIA_table.html")

```
